item.title: Editing my LLM assisted articles
item.description: There is a price to pay when you use LLM generated articles. They may be functional, but they do not capture the human experience. I'm rewriting them all.

item.title: TI-84 Evo Graphing Calculator | Texas Instruments
item.description: The TI-84 Evo is the most advanced graphing calculator in the TI-84 series. Ideal for algebra, geometry, calculus, and advanced math courses in secondary schools. Learn more.

item.title: Disable Auto-Update
item.description:
How is it possible that a feature I use every day, in an app I rely on daily, entirely offline, just disappeared from my phone?
I use a fitness app. My metrics, such as steps, workout routines, heart

item.title: Why I Don’t Vibe Code
item.description: A “brief” accounting of various reasons why vibe coding has just never clicked for me personally as a developer.

item.title: Strong types are a non-negotiable for LLMs | HireUp
item.description: Why HireUp leans hard on the type system to let AI agents ship features quickly and confidently.

item.title: Using “underdrawings” for accurate text and numbers
item.description: A technique for accurate text and numbers in AI-generated images: generate the layout deterministically, then ask the image model to paint on top.
item.author: (Sam Collins)

item.title: A Sum of Errors
item.description: Or, how to lie with numeric affordances

item.title: Running hackathons: startup&scaleup edition
item.description: Hackathons are a type of drug - once an addict, always an addict. Here’s my internal hackathon playbook for every startup/scaleup.

item.title: The resonance of Berkshire Hathaway
item.description: Brands can be simple. Why Warren Buffett's company stands as an example of low-key brilliance.

item.title: Evolving Agent Memory — BenEmson.com
item.description: How elfmem builds agent memory with a self, mind blocks for simulating other minds, and a peer protocol so agents in different projects can talk to each other.

item.title: Budget LTE Home Network | World of Matthew
item.description: My Blog

item.title: There’s No Minimum Angel Check Size
item.description: Written pieces, talks, and other bits by Zach Holman.

item.title: Product market fit isn't a stage, it's a gauntlet — jonno.nz
item.description: PMF gets sold as a milestone. It's actually a gauntlet that bends founders, breaks teams, and quietly poisons technical decisions. Most of the damage isn't from missing PMF — it's from how you behave while looking for it.
item.author: (John Gregoriadis)

item.title: KV Cache Locality: The Hidden Variable in Your LLM Serving Cost
item.description: Every time your load balancer sends a request to the wrong GPU, that GPU recomputes a prefill it already computed somewhere else. The KV cache for that 4,000-token system prompt exists. It’s just sitting on a different card. Your load balancer doesn’t know. It can’t know. It’s counting connections, not tokens.

item.title: If Claude Writes the Code, What Makes Me Still a Developer?
item.description: It’s been three months since I last wrote a line of code. I still deliver features. I still solve problems, tackle architecture challenges, review PRs. I’m not a PM. I’m not a tech lead with a team of agents. I still consider myself a developer. But I am not doing the same job I was doing a year ago. The gradient, not the cliff There was no single moment where I thought “this isn’t the same job anymore.

item.title: Ryan Bigg - NPM: putting the brown in brownout
item.description: NPM: putting the brown in brownout

item.title: Spicadust
item.description: minimalistic software

item.title: Cloud Skills Are Still Just Skills
item.description: I A/B tested /ultrareview against my own pr-review skill. The ultra features add a verification step. That's it. The opacity is the product, not the skill.

item.title: I Have Lived in your Camp
item.description:
You think you know me based on text on a screen. Within the context of a few blog posts, you believe to know my life history, my presumed entitlement, my naivety, my background, and my sum total of wisdom.
You do not know me, but I know of you. I

item.title: You can just do things
item.description: In the age of AI, you don’t need permission to start. Here’s why you can—and should—just do things without waiting for approval.

item.title: Word Embedding is Magic!
item.description: Word embedding is a magic trick that allows computers to understand language.
item.author: (Rafi Hasan)

item.title: A Deep Dive into Email Addresses
item.description: Most people think an email address is just a username and a domain. It is a lot more than that. This covers structure, rule, edge case, and security trap worth knowing about.
item.author: (Lasan Nishshanka)

item.title: Open Source Does Not Imply Open Community
item.description: Open source software has existed long before the invention of the (D)VCS. The author likely hosted a barebones HTML webpage or a txt file describing the project. There definitely was an FTP server somewhere with tarballs. The author may have been reachable by email. If you were really lucky …
item.author: (feld)

item.title: ZetaLib/The Gay Jailbreak/The Gay Jailbreak.md at main · Exocija/ZetaLib
item.description: 🌙 ZetaLib - The only AI Library you need. Contribute to Exocija/ZetaLib development by creating an account on GitHub.

item.title: AI Water Use Distractions and Lessons for California - California WaterBlog
item.description: By Jay Lund . . . Artificial intelligence (AI) will affect many economic and natural resource sectors as these new technologies develop and mature. We are in the early years of this process. Like most new things, AI has become an object of small and great hopes and fears – from hopes for saving and

item.title: Bottle
Uber Spends Full 2026 AI Budget in 4 Months
item.description: Uber burned its entire 2026 AI budget on Claude Code and Cursor in just 4 months. Engineers' API costs ranged from $500 to $2,000.
item.author: (Andre Savage)

item.title: GitHub - darrylmorley/whatcable: macOS menu bar app that tells you, in plain English, what each USB-C cable plugged into your Mac can actually do
item.description: macOS menu bar app that tells you, in plain English, what each USB-C cable plugged into your Mac can actually do - darrylmorley/whatcable

item.title: Grok 4.3 | xAI Docs
item.description: Learn how to use our products and services

item.title: SBC Clusters are a terrible value, but they're fun anyway
item.description: Pictured above is the new DeskPi Super4C installed in an 8U mini rack. The Super4C is a 4-node Raspberry Pi CM5 cluster board that solves two pain points I had with the older Super6C. I was testing this board around the same time I helped kick off the SBCC 2026, the Single Board Cluster Competition for students. A dozen or so university teams squared off to run the best mini HPC cluster with a budget of $6,000, and a couple days to benchmark six HPC workloads.

item.title: appkittie — App Store Intelligence & Research
item.description: See any app's downloads, revenue, ads, and keywords. Track competitors, find winning niches, and grow your app on easy mode.

item.title: Microsoft open-sources "the earliest DOS source code discovered to date"
item.description: Old 86-DOS source code dates back to the time before Microsoft bought it.
